Off Calendar Sep-25-2024 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Feb-24-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion For Order That Matters In Request For Admission Of Truth Of Facts Be Deemed Admitted
Off Calendar Jul-19-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A.'S Motion For Order That Matters In Request For Admission Of Truth Of Facts Be Deemed Admitted

Plaintiff has filed a collections action against defendant in compliance with Rules 3.740 of the Rules of Court. Plaintiff's complaint alleges that defendant has maintained an open book account with the bank on which he owes $6,679.34, with the last payment on the account made over three years earlier. Defendant, acting in propria persona, filed an answer to the bank's complaint consisting of a general denial and a number of affirmative defenses.

On April 3, 2024, plaintiff served a Request for Admissions on defendant by mail. The admission requests asked defendant to admit that he had opened the identified credit account, that there was a balance due on the account of $6,679.34 and that there had not been any payments made on the account since March, 2021. To date, the defendant has not provided any responses to the requests.

Now before the Court is plaintiff's motion for an order deeming the Request for Admissions admitted pursuant to CCP sections 2023.030(b) and 2033.280(b). Defendant has not filed any response to plaintiff's motion. In its motion, the plaintiff noted it would present its motion without an appearance pursuant to Rule 3.1304(c) of the Rules of Court.

In the absence of any response or objection by defendant to the plaintiff's motion, and for good cause shown, plaintiff's motion to deem the Request for Admissions admitted is GRANTED.
Plaintiff's counsel shall prepare a form of proposed order and submit it to psw@hassard.com for review and signature.

Judge Pro Tem: Philip Ward; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JPT)
